_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-29 Mechanical Chest Compression Device (Revised 5/26/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-29: Mechanical Chest Compression Device — MDHHS Approved 5/26/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Manual CPR remains the standard of care in Michigan prehospital EMS. Mechanical chest compression devices may only be used as an alternative to conventional CPR in specific settings where high-quality manual compressions may be challenging or dangerous for the provider, such as limited rescuers, hypothermic cardiac arrest, or CPR in a moving ambulance. MCAs must specify the FDA-approved device by brand and model name. Providers must be trained. LVAD patients are contraindicated.

sections.title: Authorization and Training
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved mechanical chest compression device use
MCA must specify the FDA-approved device brand name and model in their authorization
All providers using the device must be trained per MCA requirements
Manual CPR remains the standard of care — mechanical device use does not replace the need for high-quality manual CPR when device is unavailable or inappropriate

sections.title: Indications and Contraindications
points: INDICATION: cardiac arrest in a setting where high-quality manual CPR may be challenging or dangerous, such as limited rescuers, hypothermic cardiac arrest, or CPR in a moving ambulance
CONTRAINDICATION: RETURN OF SPONTANEOUS CIRCULATION (ROSC) — discontinue device immediately upon ROSC
CONTRAINDICATION: Age or weight outside the manufacturer's specified limits for the device
CONTRAINDICATION: LVAD patients — refer to LVAD-Procedure Protocol

sections.title: Documentation
points: Document device type and brand name in PCR
Document times when device was initiated and discontinued
Document compressions rate confirmed by device (if displayed)
Document device use in relation to outcome (ROSC, TOR, continued arrest)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether mechanical chest compression devices are authorized and specify the FDA-approved brand and model
options: Authorized — device brand/model specified by MCA

deterioration_flags: ROSC achieved — STOP mechanical device IMMEDIATELY; assess pulse and hemodynamics; manage per ROSC protocol
Patient weight or size outside device manufacturer limits — use manual CPR only
LVAD patient — DO NOT use mechanical CPR device; consult Medical Control
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA specifies FDA-approved device brand and model
Manual CPR remains the standard of care
Providers must be trained on the specific authorized device
Contraindications: ROSC, age/weight outside manufacturer limits, LVAD patients
Document device type, times in use, rate
